title: Best Original Screenplay Odds - Oscar Predictions 2026 Academy Awards
text: "Sinners is the overwhelming favorite to win Best Original Screenplay at the 2026 Oscars at 97%." The remaining contenders have significantly lower odds: Marty Supreme at 3%, Sentimental Value at 2%, and both It Was Just an Accident and Blue Moon at 1%. Expert Analysis: Collin Whitchurch, the content manager at Action Network, explains that the Ryan Coogler film dominates this category due to its unprecedented precursor wins. The screenplay has captured awards from the BAFTAs, Critics Choice, WGA, and NBR—the only notable exception being the Golden Globes, where it faced different competition. Whitchurch notes that "the early season favorite in this category was Sentimental Value" but Sinners has since overtaken it significantly. He also highlights the historical significance: "Jordan Peele became the first Black winner of a screenplay Oscar for Get Out nearly a decade ago. Coogler is likely to be the second." The Verdict: The expert's prediction is straightforward: there is no value in betting here, as Sinners will almost certainly claim the award. The dominance of this film across every major precursor ceremony makes this perhaps the most predictable category of the entire evening.
